name: Agent4Edu Evaluates Generated Learner Responses Against Human Response Consistency
statement: Agent4Edu evaluates generated learner responses by measuring consistency and discrepancies relative to human learner responses, reporting ACC, F1-score, and ROUGE-3 learner-simulation metrics.
population_or_context: Personalized learning and computerized adaptive testing simulation.
time_horizon: arXiv page submitted 2025-01-17, revised 2026-05-28, retrieved on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The staged extraction records 18,045 response records from 500 Chinese high school students and reports Agent4Edu (GPT-3.5-turbo) at 66.70 ACC, 79.84 F1-score, and 37.97 ROUGE-3.
caveats: The evaluation measures simulator response fidelity rather than scorer validity.
Learner response records and personalized learning interactions differ from rubric-scored constructed responses.
evaluation_method: Consistency and discrepancy assessment between generated agent responses and human learner responses.
reported_metrics: ACC, F1-score, and ROUGE-3 for learner simulation.
limitations: Appendix prompt-level details need further review before finer prompt claims.
The generated response quality evidence is simulator-specific.
This source does not establish synthetic responses as validation data for an automated scorer.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding paraphrases the method and experiment sections describing learner profiles, response generation, dataset size, model details, and ACC/F1/ROUGE evaluation.
